title: Method and systems for controlling an update of devices
abstract: To control an update of machine-readable data processed by devices communicatively coupled to a Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system (60), a fleet management system (80) communicatively interfaces with the Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system (60).
claims:
Claims 1. A method of controlling an update of machine-readable data processed by devices (41-46; 120) communicatively coupled to a Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition, SCADA, system (60), the method comprising: determining, by a fleet management, FLM, system (80), a device set (121) of the devices, wherein the device set (121) comprises devices to be updated; communicatively interfacing, by the FLM system (80), with the SCADA system (60) to establish an update sequence for the devices included in the device set (121); triggering, by the FLM system (80), update processes for the devices included in the device set (121) based on the established update sequence. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein communicatively interfacing by the FLM system (80) with the SCADA system (60) to establish the update sequence comprises: generating, by the FLM system (80), first data comprising information on the devices to be updated included in the device set (121); outputting, by the FLM system (80), the first data via at least one data interface; receiving, by the FLM system (80), second data from the SCADA system (60) via the at least one data interface, the second data comprising information on at least a subset (122-124) of the device set (121); and establishing, by the FLM system (80), the update sequence based on the second data. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first data comprises identifiers for all devices to be updated. 4. The method of claim 2 or claim 3, wherein the first data comprises estimated update durations. 5. The method of any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein the second data defines an ordered list of several subsets (122-124) of the devices to be updated to define the update sequence. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the second data comprises a plurality of messages received from the SCADA system (60), wherein each of the messages defines one of the subsets (122- 7. The method of claim 6, wherein receipt of a message of the plurality of messages causes the FLM system (80) to trigger the update process for the subset (122-124) defined by the message. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein determining, by the FLM system (80) communicatively interfacing with the SCADA system (60), the update sequence comprises determining which of the devices to be updated will remain in maintenance mode for at least an estimated update duration. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein determining, by the FLM system (80) communicatively interfacing with the SCADA system (60), the update sequence comprises determining for which of the devices to be updated there is a redundant implementation of all safety-critical functions performed by the respective devices. 10. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein communicatively interfacing by the FLM system (80) with the SCADA system (60) to establish the update sequence comprises: retrieving, by the FLM system (80), device status data for the devices included in the device set (121) from a storage system of the SCADA system (60) or accessible to the SCADA system (60), wherein the FLM system (80) establishes the update sequence based on the retrieved device status data. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: receiving, by the FLM system (80), update reports via the at least one data interface; and providing, by the FLM system (80), an aggregated update report aggregated from the received update reports to the SCADA system (60). 12. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the update sequence is determined automatically by the FLM system (80) cooperating with the SCADA system (60). 13. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the devices comprise devices of an automation control system (40). 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the automation control system (40) comprises an electric power system automation control system, and wherein the devices comprise devices of the electric power system automation control system. 15. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the update comprises a firmware update. 16. A fleet management, FLM, system (80) for controlling an update of machine-readable data processed by devices (41-46; 120) communicatively coupled with a Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition, SCADA, system (60), the FLM system (80) comprising: at least one data interface (81); and at least one processing circuit (83) operative to determine a device set (121) of the devices, wherein the device set (121) comprises devices to be updated; control the at least one data interface (81) to communicatively interface the FLM system (80) with the SCADA system (60) to establish an update sequence for the devices included in the device set (121); trigger update processes for the devices included in the device set (121) based on the established update sequence. 17. The FLM system (80) of claim 16, operative to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 15. 18. An electric power system, comprising: an automation control system (40) comprising a plurality of devices (41-46; 120) operative to process machine-readable data; a Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition, SCADA, system (60) operative to be communicatively coupled to the plurality of devices (41-46; 120); and the FLM system (80) of claim 16 or claim 17 operative to be communicatively coupled to the SCADA system (60). 19. The electric power system of claim 18, wherein the FLM system (80) is operative to generate first data comprising information on the devices to be updated included in the device set (121), 

 transmit the first data to the SCADA system (60), receive second data from the SCADA system (60), the second data comprising information on several subsets (122-124) of the device set (121), and establish the update sequence based on the second data; and wherein the SCADA system (60) is operative to receive the first data, retrieve, based on the information on the devices to be updated included in the first data, device status data for at least the devices to be updated, determine, based on the device status data, the several subsets (122-124) of the device set (121) to specify the update sequence, generate the second data based on the determined several subsets (122-124), and transmit the second data to the FLM system (80).
